sourceMovie.title: The Lost Bladesman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Lost Bladesman works as a history movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Braveheart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lost Bladesman clicked for you, Braveheart (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lost Bladesman?
faq.answer: Try Braveheart, Zulu, Red Cliff II—each shares DNA with The Lost Bladesman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lost Bladesman?
faq.answer: The Lost Bladesman (2011) is best described as History, Drama, Action, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lost Bladesman worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
